text.title: FinOps — Rightsizing + Graviton Migration Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the fco-rightsizing methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Rightsizing recommendation MUST be backed by ≥ 4 weeks of CPU + memory + network utilization data; sub-4-week recommendations are forbidden.

rationale.source: AWS Compute Optimizer docs, FinOps Foundation

rule.rationale: Shorter baselines miss weekly cycles, batch jobs, monthly reports; 4 weeks is the empirical floor.

rule.statement: Recommended instance MUST have ≥ 20% headroom over the workload's p95 CPU + p95 memory; sizing to p50 is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Sizing to p50 produces frequent saturation events; the 20% headroom over p95 covers normal variance.

rule.statement: Before recommending x86 sizing, the workload MUST be evaluated for Graviton migration; eligible workloads MUST be recommended Graviton with ~20-40% additional savings.

rationale.source: AWS Graviton docs, AWS Graviton Ready partners

rule.rationale: Graviton offers the largest single-step compute saving currently available; missing it leaves money on the table.

rule.statement: Downsizing MUST account for burst capacity (T-series credits, short-lived batch jobs); downsizing into credit depletion is forbidden.
rule.rationale: T-instance credit exhaustion produces throttle events that look like an outage; the savings disappear under support cost.

rule.statement: Rightsizing changes MUST roll out via blue/green or canary; in-place stop/resize/start on production is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Stop/resize/start incurs downtime; canary catches sizing regressions before they hit all users.

rule.statement: If workload has < 4 weeks of utilization data OR is bound to x86-only commercial software, this methodology MUST be skipped (collect baseline first OR use commercial vendor's sizing guide).
rule.rationale: Insufficient data or hard constraint makes the recommendation noise.
